speaker: TALBOT
text: O young John Talbot, I did send for thee

speaker: TALBOT
text: To tutor thee in stratagems of war,

speaker: TALBOT
text: That Talbot’s name might be in thee revived

speaker: TALBOT
text: When sapless age and weak unable limbs

speaker: TALBOT
text: Should bring thy father to his drooping chair.

speaker: TALBOT
text: But—O, malignant and ill-boding stars!—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Now thou art come unto a feast of Death,

speaker: TALBOT
text: A terrible and unavoided danger.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Therefore, dear boy, mount on my swiftest horse,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And I’ll direct thee how thou shalt escape

speaker: TALBOT
text: By sudden flight. Come, dally not, be gone.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Is my name Talbot? And am I your son?

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: And shall I fly? O, if you love my mother,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Dishonor not her honorable name

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: To make a bastard and a slave of me!

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: The world will say “He is not Talbot’s blood,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: That basely fled when noble Talbot stood.”

speaker: TALBOT
text: Fly, to revenge my death if I be slain.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: He that flies so will ne’er return again.

speaker: TALBOT
text: If we both stay, we both are sure to die.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Then let me stay and, father, do you fly.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Your loss is great; so your regard should be.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: My worth unknown, no loss is known in me.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Upon my death, the French can little boast;

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: In yours they will; in you all hopes are lost.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Flight cannot stain the honor you have won,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: But mine it will, that no exploit have done.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: You fled for vantage, everyone will swear;

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: But if I bow, they’ll say it was for fear.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: There is no hope that ever I will stay

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: If the first hour I shrink and run away.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Here on my knee I beg mortality,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Rather than life preserved with infamy.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Shall all thy mother’s hopes lie in one tomb?

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Ay, rather than I’ll shame my mother’s womb.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Upon my blessing I command thee go.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: To fight I will, but not to fly the foe.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Part of thy father may be saved in thee.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: No part of him but will be shame in me.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Thou never hadst renown, nor canst not lose it.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Yes, your renownèd name; shall flight abuse it?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Thy father’s charge shall clear thee from that stain.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: You cannot witness for me, being slain.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: If death be so apparent, then both fly.

speaker: TALBOT
text: And leave my followers here to fight and die?

speaker: TALBOT
text: My age was never tainted with such shame.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: And shall my youth be guilty of such blame?

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: No more can I be severed from your side

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Than can yourself yourself in twain divide.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Stay, go, do what you will; the like do I,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: For live I will not, if my father die.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Then here I take my leave of thee, fair son,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Born to eclipse thy life this afternoon.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Come, side by side, together live and die,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And soul with soul from France to heaven fly.
